    an evidence category that the active claim record already shows as satisfied.

    material belongs to the active category. These notes do not define carrier-specific
    accepted documents and do not identify the current request category.

args.query: site:ups.com claims supporting documents damaged package proof of value invoice receipt UPS claim

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Supporting Documents for Claims | UPS - United States

  - url: https://www.ups.com/us/en/support/file-a-claim/supporting-documents

  - source: UPS

  - domain: ups.com


  ## Content


  # Supporting Documents Required for UPS Claims


  UPS explains that claim processing depends on supporting information that matches
  the issue being reviewed. Claim paperwork is handled through the Claims Dashboard
  rather than by fax or postal mail.


  ## Help Us Help You


  Every claim requires some form of supporting documentation so UPS can locate a missing
  package or reach an accurate resolution. One example is an invoice that shows the
  value of merchandise that was lost or damaged.


  A merchandise description should be specific enough to identify the goods. Useful
  details can include serial number, brand, size, color, quantity, product number,
  model, container type, or other identifying characteristics. For some merchandise,
  especially certain electronics over $500, a serial number may be required for the
  investigation. If required identifying information is missing, the investigation
  may be closed and later reopened when the information is supplied.


  UPS also states that damage claims require photo documentation, and a physical inspection
  at a UPS-approved location may be needed. A claim can be delayed or denied when
  there is not enough supporting documentation.


  ## Merchandise Description Examples


  For clothing, useful details include the brand name, color, and item size.


  For health and beauty products, useful details include the brand, product name,
  product size, and container type.


  For office supplies, books, and picture frames, useful details include product number,
  brand, product type, and quantity.


  For tires and auto parts, useful details include item number, brand, model, product
  number, identification number, and serial number.


  For electronics, useful details include item number, brand, model, product number,
  identification number, and serial number.


  ## Photo Documentation


  When photo documentation is requested, UPS lists three required photos:


  1. A photo showing the damaged item and how it was packaged inside the box.

  2. A close-up photo of the shipping label with the tracking number, which generally
  starts with 1Z.

  3. A photo of the outside of the package showing any damage.


  UPS also asks for the package dimensions: height, length, and width.


  ## Accepted File Formats


  The claims application supports JPG, JPEG, TIF, TIFF, BMP, PDF, DOCX, and PNG files.

  '
metadata: {}

args.question: Please provide the value-substantiation document for the damaged merchandise—preferably an invoice or receipt showing the item(s) and their value. If you do not have one, tell me what proof of value you have available (for example, a sales record or other valuation document) so I can prepare the appropriate submission.

args.question: Please provide the value-substantiation document for the damaged merchandise—preferably an invoice or receipt showing the item(s) and their value. If you do not have one, tell me what proof of value you have available (for example, a sales record or other valuation document) so I can prepare the appropriate submission.
args.reason: The active UPS request (UPS-CLM-DOC-81742) is open for value substantiation only. Physical-damage evidence is already satisfied and the merchandise description is already on file, so I need the value document before staging the supporting-material request.

content.content: I need user clarification or authorization before continuing, so I will stop here until the user responds.

For this claim, send **one document proving the value of the damaged merchandise**, such as:

- The original **purchase invoice or receipt**
- A **sales invoice/order record** showing the item description and price
- A **commercial invoice** or other valuation document showing the merchandise value

The document should identify the merchandise clearly and show its value. You do **not** need to resend damage photos—the claim already has physical-damage evidence, and the merchandise description is already on file.

Please provide or upload the invoice/receipt (you may redact unrelated personal or payment details). Once you provide it, I can stage it for the active UPS request **UPS-CLM-DOC-81742**.

final_answer:
For this claim, send **one document proving the value of the damaged merchandise**, such as:

- The original **purchase invoice or receipt**
- A **sales invoice/order record** showing the item description and price
- A **commercial invoice** or other valuation document showing the merchandise value

The document should identify the merchandise clearly and show its value. You do **not** need to resend damage photos—the claim already has physical-damage evidence, and the merchandise description is already on file.

Please provide or upload the invoice/receipt (you may redact unrelated personal or payment details). Once you provide it, I can stage it for the active UPS request **UPS-CLM-DOC-81742**.
